name: Rijksmuseum
description: The Netherlands' premier art and history museum. Home to Rembrandt's Night Watch, Vermeer's Milkmaid, and an unparalleled collection of Dutch Golden Age masterpieces.

name: Van Gogh Museum
description: The world's largest collection of Van Gogh works. Over 200 paintings and 500 drawings tracing the artist's turbulent life. Book well in advance.

name: Anne Frank House
description: The secret annex where Anne Frank and her family hid during WWII. A profoundly moving historical site. Timed-entry tickets required.

name: Canal Cruise
description: Iconic boat tour through Amsterdam's UNESCO-listed canal belt. Daytime and evening options available, with hop-on hop-off variants for flexible exploring.

name: Vondelpark
description: Amsterdam's beloved urban park. Rent a bike and cycle its paths, join locals for a picnic, or catch an open-air theatre performance in summer.

name: Stedelijk Museum
description: World-class modern and contemporary art museum housed in a striking building. Strong collection of Mondrian, Malevich, and rotating international exhibitions.

name: Jordaan Neighbourhood Walk
description: Self-guided stroll through Amsterdam's most charming neighbourhood. Narrow bridges, independent galleries, artisan coffee shops, and hidden hofjes (courtyards).

name: Heineken Experience
description: Interactive brewery tour inside the original Heineken factory. Includes tastings, interactive exhibits, and a souvenir glass. Popular with groups.

name: Day Trip to Keukenhof
description: The world's largest flower garden, open March-May. Over 7 million bulbs. Combine with a visit to the nearby windmills at Kinderdijk. Seasonal.

name: FOAM Photography Museum
description: Intimate photography museum in a beautiful canal house. Rotating exhibitions featuring both emerging and established photographers from around the world.

name: Louvre Museum
description: The world's most visited museum. Home to the Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo, and over 35,000 works spanning millennia. Arrive early to beat the crowds.

name: Eiffel Tower
description: Paris's defining icon. Take the stairs for the best experience, or queue for the glass-floor summit. Magical at dusk when the twinkling lights begin.

name: Musée d'Orsay
description: Housed in a converted Belle Époque railway station, this is the world's finest Impressionist collection. Monets, Degases, Renoirs, and Van Goghs under one roof.

name: Montmartre Walk
description: Walk the winding lanes of Paris's artistic hilltop village. Visit the Sacré-Cœur, the original Moulin Rouge, and the vineyard. Best at sunrise or sunset.

name: Seine River Cruise
description: Classic Paris experience. A Bateaux Mouches or Vedettes du Pont Neuf cruise past Notre-Dame, the Louvre, and the Eiffel Tower. Evening sailings are especially romantic.

name: Centre Pompidou
description: Inside-out architectural marvel housing Europe's largest modern art museum. Strong Picasso, Matisse, and Kandinsky collections plus cutting-edge temporary shows.

name: Versailles Day Trip
description: The Sun King's impossibly grand palace and 800-hectare gardens. Buy a passport ticket to access the Grand Trianon and Marie Antoinette's estate. Full day minimum.

name: Le Marais Food Walk
description: Guided or self-guided food walk through the historic Marais. L'As du Fallafel, Pierre Hermé macarons, and artisanal cheese shops are essential stops.

name: Sagrada Família
description: Gaudí's life's work and Spain's most visited monument. Still under construction after 140 years. The interior is a kaleidoscopic forest of light. Book months ahead.

name: Park Güell
description: Fantastical Gaudí park with mosaic terraces and gingerbread gatehouses. The monumental zone requires timed tickets. Views over Barcelona are superb.

name: Gothic Quarter
description: Barcelona's ancient heart. Wander past Roman ruins, medieval churches, and narrow lanes unchanged since the Middle Ages. The Barcelona Cathedral anchors it.

name: Picasso Museum
description: Housed in five connected medieval palaces. Chronicles Picasso's formative years in Barcelona. Wonderful Blue Period and early masterpieces.

name: La Boqueria Market
description: Barcelona's most famous covered market on Las Ramblas. Vibrant stalls of jamón, seafood, fruit, and spices. Arrive before 10am to beat the tour groups.

name: Barceloneta Beach
description: The city beach minutes from the Gothic Quarter. Golden sand, beach volleyball, and dozens of chiringuitos (beach bars) serving cold cava and grilled fish.

name: Montjuïc and MNAC
description: Take the cable car or funicular to Montjuïc hill. The MNAC museum houses a world-class Romanesque art collection. The Olympic stadium and fantastic Barcelona panoramas.

name: El Born Tapas Tour
description: Bar-hop through El Born's best pintxos and tapas bars. Local vermouth, jamón ibérico, and patatas bravas. Essential for first-time visitors.

name: Colosseum and Roman Forum
description: The world's most iconic ancient monument. Book a gladiator school or underground tour for the best experience. Combined ticket includes Palatine Hill.

name: Vatican Museums and Sistine Chapel
description: One of the world's great art collections housed in the Apostolic Palace. The Sistine Chapel ceiling is the climax. Book skip-the-line tickets obligatory.

name: Colosseum
description: The Flavian Amphitheatre, heart of ancient Roman spectacle. Combined tickets available with the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. Guided tours highly recommended.

name: Trevi Fountain
description: Bernini's greatest fountain. Toss a coin to ensure your return. Visit very early morning or late at night to experience it without crowds.

name: Borghese Gallery
description: One of Rome's finest museums limited to 360 visitors at a time. Bernini's virtuoso sculptures and Caravaggio's explosive canvases. Advance booking essential.

name: Trastevere Evening Walk
description: Rome's most romantic neighbourhood comes alive after dark. Golden evening light on terracotta facades, open-air dining, and jazz spilling from bars.

name: Day Trip to Pompeii
description: Take the Trenitalia high-speed to Naples then circumvesuviana to Pompeii. Walk the eerie preserved streets of the city frozen in 79AD. Full day essential.

name: Pantheon
description: The best-preserved ancient Roman building. The unreinforced concrete dome is still an engineering marvel after 2,000 years. Free entry on certain days.

name: Belém Tower
description: UNESCO-listed 16th-century fortress on the Tagus estuary. One of Lisbon's most photographed symbols. Combine with the nearby Jerónimos Monastery.

name: Jerónimos Monastery
description: Portugal's greatest Gothic-Manueline monument, built to celebrate Vasco da Gama's voyages. The maritime cloister is extraordinary.

name: Alfama Fado Show
description: Authentic fado performance in a traditional casa de fado in the Alfama. Dinner and wine included. Book in advance for the best venues like Clube de Fado.

name: Tram 28 and Alfama Walk
description: Ride the iconic yellow tram through the historic Alfama, Graça, and Estrela neighbourhoods. Get off at São Jorge Castle for sweeping city and river views.

name: MAAT Museum
description: The Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology on the Tagus riverfront. A striking contemporary building with international art shows and a rooftop terrace.

name: Sintra Day Trip
description: A fairy-tale UNESCO World Heritage town 40 minutes from Lisbon by train. Explore Pena Palace, the Moorish Castle, and the Quinta da Regaleira. Arrive early.

name: LX Factory Sunday Market
description: Lisbon's most creative weekend market inside a converted 19th-century factory. Local designers, vintage clothing, street food, and the best pastel de nata in the city.

name: Oceanarium
description: One of Europe's finest oceanariums, designed for the 1998 Expo. A single central tank mimics the open ocean, surrounded by four smaller regional tanks. Great for families.
